name: Eric Clapton
abstract: Eric Patrick Clapton (born 30 March 1945) is an English rock and blues guitarist, singer, and songwriter. He is regarded as one of the most successful and influential guitarists in rock music.

concerts.label: Benefit: Wyclef Jean Foundation
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: people with incomes like Eric Clapton and Paul McCartney can afford
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Luis Conte
about_quotes.evidence: I remember I played on an Eric Clapton song, it was called “Change the World”... Eric was going to come in and play the guitar. And here’s an example of a great producer... So I went in there and did my thing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luis Conte interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 24, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: The guitarists were Bo Diddley, Jimmy Ray Vaughan, Eric Clapton... I was really glad to be a part of that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chris Parker
about_quotes.evidence: there were a lot of people like Leo Sayer or Eric Clapton or Elvis Costello or Linda Ronstadt, Aaron Neville, where they didn’t have a band so they used a put together band from New York or they used certain members of the Saturday Night band itself to do their performance.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chris Parker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 5, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Conniff
about_quotes.evidence: He had Eric Clapton on. And he was playing guitar, and I think except the way I heard him and his writings with the guitar, mainly today...
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Conniff interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Pallo
about_quotes.evidence: Clapton, Eric Clapton I never had an opportunity to do anything with him, meaning like either a session or have him sit in.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Pallo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2007
